news.title: New buildings, boundary walls found on protected forest land in Anangpur despite SC curbs
news.para: Fresh construction, including a multi-storey building and boundary walls, has emerged in Anangpur village's protected forest belt, a PLPA-notified area. This development occurs despite Supreme Court interventions and findings of thousands of illegal structures in the Aravalis. Experts warn that such activity fragments the ecosystem, disrupts groundwater recharge, and blocks wildlife corridors, signaling continued pressure on this ecologically vital region.

news.title: Jaipur development body turns to direct plot auctions to boost revenue
news.para: In response to a real estate slump, the Jaipur Development Authority (JDA) is now directly auctioning residential plots to end buyers, a departure from its previous investor-centric approach. This strategic shift aims to boost auction participation and revenue by offering vacant plots in schemes like Vipra Vihar and Bilwa. The JDA plans to extend these direct sales to several other housing projects, targeting individual homebuyers.

news.title: Over 260 LEDs to rescue Panchkula villages from permanent nights
news.para: Panchkula's rural residents are set to receive much-needed street lighting as the development and panchayat department has tendered ₹11.72 lakh for 261 LED lights across ten villages.  This initiative aims to curb night-time accidents and deter wild animals, addressing long-standing safety concerns.  Years of administrative delays are finally being overcome with a fresh, streamlined tender, bringing relief to communities who have actively campaigned for this essential infrastructure.

news.title: Bhubaneswar: BDA orders allotees’ association to conduct fresh election for executive panel
news.para: Bhubaneswar Development Authority has ordered fresh elections for a Kalinga Nagar apartment project's executive committee due to serious procedural violations. The previous October 2024 election was invalidated for failing to publish a final voter list and lacking the required quorum. This marks a significant enforcement action under the new Odisha Apartment Act, signaling stricter oversight of residents' associations.

news.title: Indore civic body to invite tenders for construction of 8,100 PMAY 2.0 flats
news.para: Indore Municipal Corporation is set to build 8,100 new flats under the PMAY scheme, costing around Rs 1,200 crore. This initiative aims to rehouse families affected by road widening and infrastructure projects, while also boosting affordable housing.  A portion of the flats will be 1-BHK for displaced residents, with larger 2, 3, and 4-BHK units sold at market rates to offset costs.

news.title: RWA can’t stop essential services to recover dues: Haryana's registrar-general
news.para: Haryana's registrar-general has ruled that Resident Welfare Associations (RWAs) cannot halt essential services like garbage collection or digital access to recover unpaid maintenance dues. Furthermore, maintenance charges must be determined by house size, not imposed uniformly on all properties. This decision arose from a dispute in Mayfield Gardens, clarifying RWA powers and ensuring fair billing practices for residents.

news.title: Fresh survey to focus on existing built-up areas, not new claims in Haryana
news.para: Authorities are conducting a ground-truthing exercise in Faridabad's Anangpur to identify residential areas within PLPA land. To ensure accuracy, fresh constructions or expansions made during the survey will not be included in the assessment. This verification, directed by the Supreme Court's CEC, aims to document existing development and clarify long-pending issues regarding settlements, preventing attempts to alter property status during the process.

news.title: CREDAI backs CRZ reclassification plan for Visakhapatnam coast
news.para: Visakhapatnam's coastline faces a potential reclassification from CRZ-III B to CRZ-II, impacting nearly 499 hectares. Officials propose this change to boost urban development, including a coastal corridor and airport connectivity, attracting real estate investment. However, environmental groups voice concerns about increased construction and its effect on coastal ecosystems. The proposal awaits approval from the Union environment ministry.

news.title: Odisha RERA urges government for greater autonomy
news.para: Odisha's real estate authority is pushing the state government for greater financial and administrative independence. Citing operational hurdles, ORERA highlighted its dependence on government approvals for staff recruitment and salary disbursal, impacting its ability to handle increasing complaints. This move aligns with central government advice to bolster state regulators, aiming to enhance efficiency, speed up grievance redressal, and boost consumer confidence in the real estate sector.

news.title: NHB probing Aavas Financiers over loan classification lapses
news.para: The sector regulator has recalled refinancing support worth nearly Rs 500 crore —a punitive action that has set off a sweeping leadership overhaul at the company. ET was the first to report on April 13 that managing director and CEO Sachinder Bhinder was being asked to step down, with Manu Singh — former home loans head at Kotak Mahindra Bank — set to take over. A week later, on April 20, the company confirmed Bhinder's resignation and Singh's appointment as the new CEO.

news.title: Gurugram civic body plans new construction waste plant by February 2027
news.para: Gurugram is set to significantly boost its construction and demolition waste management with a new 400-tonne-per-day processing plant slated for 2027. The city is expanding its collection network, with new centres opening soon.  MCG aims to sell a substantial amount of processed waste to both government and private sectors, while also planning public awareness campaigns to encourage its use in municipal projects.

news.title: Ahmedabad civic body to waive property tax on parking spaces if malls, hotels open them to public
news.para: Ahmedabad Municipal Corporation is offering a 100% property tax exemption on parking areas to commercial establishments in exchange for opening their private basements to the public. This initiative aims to clear congested streets, following high court directives. Businesses have 60 days to comply, with potential new taxes on parking areas if they refuse. Concerns regarding security and customer displacement are being addressed with flexible proposals.

news.title: Telangana government cracks down on illegal construction & demolition dumping
news.para: Hyderabad's government is cracking down on illegal construction waste dumping. New rules now mandate developers to manage demolition debris responsibly, requiring undertakings against illegal dumping and proof of recycling partnerships. Large construction projects and road works must now incorporate recycled C&D waste, promoting sustainable practices and cleaner urban spaces across key municipal areas.

news.title: Maharashtra: Vertical property cards proposal awaits legal clearance, likely to be discussed in assembly session
news.para: Maharashtra is poised to introduce vertical property cards for apartment owners, a move expected to be discussed in the upcoming state legislature session. This groundbreaking initiative aims to provide individual ownership records for flats, enhancing transparency and simplifying transactions.  Currently awaiting final legal clearance, the proposal, if approved, will create a separate revenue record for each unit, marking a significant modernization of property records.

news.title: MahaRERA must use tech, AI to track, audit project compliance: Lawyers
news.para: MahaRERA's penalties on developers for delayed reports raise concerns about its oversight. Experts argue the regulator lacks mechanisms to verify developer disclosures, suggesting random inspections and AI monitoring. While MahaRERA prioritizes grievance redressal, critics point to potential loopholes in fund protection and the need for independent verification of project progress and financial statements to safeguard homebuyers.

news.title: Gurugram: 181 PG rooms, 29 commercial units sealed in DLF phase-III
news.para: Authorities in Gurugram have intensified a crackdown on illegal constructions in DLF Phase-3, sealing 181 rooms in paying guest accommodations and 29 commercial establishments operating from residential properties. The drive, complying with high court directives, aims to restore buildings to their original sanctioned plans and remove unauthorized commercial activities, including those in stilt parking areas.

news.title: Tech Mahindra leases four lakh sq ft office space in Hyderabad
news.para: In a major real estate development, Tech Mahindra has secured 4 lakh sq ft of office space in Hyderabad with a 10-year lease, reinforcing the city's position as a tech hub. This decision reflects the vibrant demand for premium office spaces among technology firms. The company's investment highlights Hyderabad's attractiveness as a primary location for large corporations looking for seamless expansion and reliable long-term occupancy.

news.title: Japan's NIFCO leases 5.13-acre plot in Maharashtra for manufacturing plant
news.para: NIFCO India is expanding its manufacturing capabilities with a new 5.13-acre plant in Shendra MIDC, Chhatrapati Sambhaji Nagar. This marks the automotive component maker's fourth facility in India, aiming to meet rising domestic demand from car manufacturers. The move underscores India's strategic importance for NIFCO and highlights the growing interest from Japanese firms in the country's industrial sector.

news.title: Maharashtra launches drive to correct leasehold land records, collectors to correct records within three months
news.para: Revenue Minister Chandrashekhar Bawankule has ordered a statewide overhaul of government leasehold land records. This initiative aims to correct erroneous entries where leaseholders were wrongly listed as 'occupants,' enabling illegal ownership claims. District collectors must update records within three months, ensuring the state government is exclusively marked as the occupant, with lease details in a separate column. This move seeks to enhance transparency and prevent land misappropriation.

news.title: Dalmia Bharat plans to raise ₹4,000 crore; targets 110-130 MTPA cement capacity by FY31
news.para: Dalmia Bharat is set to raise up to Rs 4,000 crore to fuel its ambitious expansion, aiming to boost manufacturing capacity to 110-130 million tonnes per annum by FY31. This strategic move, involving acquisitions and organic growth, aligns with projected industry demand driven by infrastructure and housing. The company is also focusing on premium products and expanding its pan-India presence, reinforcing its commitment to national development.
